title: Cheirolophus arbutifolius
intro: Cheirolophus arbutifolius, also known as the White Rock Rose or the Madeira Rockrose, is a flowering plant species native to the island of Madeira, Portugal. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Cheirolophus arbutifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cheirolophus arbutifolius is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever and cough.
taxonomy: Cheirolophus arbutifolius is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is a small to medium-sized shrub native to the Macaronesian islands. The genus Cheirolophus is a part of the tribe Senecioneae, which includes other species of daisies and related plants. Cheirolophus arbutifolius is closely related to other island-endemic species in the same genus. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as an isolated island species.
uses: Cheirolophus arbutifolius has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their possible use in treating various health conditions, including digestive issues and infections.
